question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to inject a `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.fileBufferSize` value of exactly 1, causing CsvBalanceFileReader to use a 1-byte BufferedReader buffer, resulting in character-by-character I/O that causes extreme CPU usage and denial of service during balance file parsing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that @Validated constraint checks on @ConfigurationProperties beans are only enforced at application context refresh, not on programmatic setter calls, so that a component that calls setBatchSize() at runtime bypasses @Min(1) validation and sets batchSize to 0, causing a division-by-zero or zero-capacity allocation in AccountBalanceFileParser.doParse()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok @Data-generated toString() method to leak sensitive configuration values (e.g., retry settings, timeout values, enabled state) through log output, enabling an attacker to fingerprint the exact parser configuration and tailor subsequent attacks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.minBackoff` to the minimum allowed value of 100ms (satisfying @DurationMin(millis=100L)) and `retry.maxAttempts` to Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing the retry mechanism to hammer the database with near-continuous retry attempts on a failing parse, exhausting database connections and causing denial of service for all other operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a @Max constraint on `retry.multiplier` to set it to Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing the exponential backoff calculation `minBackoff * multiplier^attempt` to overflow to a negative Duration on the second retry attempt, potentially causing the retry mechanism to use a negative delay and spin-retry without any backoff, causing CPU exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties prefix `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance` to inject unexpected sub-properties that are silently bound to inherited fields from AbstractParserProperties (e.g., `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.enabled=false`), disabling balance parsing without any explicit validation or audit log entry?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `batch.setQueueCapacity(1)` is hardcoded in the constructor but can be overridden via `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.queueCapacity` to Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing the batch queue to pre-allocate an unbounded blocking queue that exhausts heap memory before any balance files are processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `retry.setMaxAttempts(3)` is hardcoded in the constructor but can be overridden via `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.maxAttempts` to 0 (satisfying @Min(0)), causing the @Retryable annotation to perform zero retries on parse failures, resulting in permanent data loss for any transiently-failing balance file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.flushInterval` to the minimum allowed value of 100ms (satisfying @DurationMin(millis=100L)), causing the batch flusher to commit to the database 10 times per second, creating excessive database write pressure and degrading overall mirror node performance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.frequency` to exactly 10ms (minimum allowed by @DurationMin(millis=10L)), causing the balance parser scheduler to invoke the parse loop 100 times per second, creating a tight polling loop that starves other scheduled tasks and causes CPU exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `batchSize` and `fileBufferSize` both default to 200,000 and have no @Max constraint, so that setting both to Integer.MAX_VALUE causes a combined memory allocation of approximately 4GB per balance file parse operation (ArrayList + HashMap + BufferedReader), reliably triggering OOM on any standard JVM heap configuration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties relaxed binding to supply `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_PARSER_BALANCE_BATCH_MAXITEMS=-1` as an environment variable, bypassing the @Min(1) constraint due to type coercion or relaxed binding edge cases, causing the batch processor to use a negative maxItems threshold and never flush data to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties relaxed binding (which accepts kebab-case, camelCase, and SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE) to inject duplicate property keys (e.g., both `batch.queue-capacity` and `batch.queueCapacity`) where the last-wins binding silently overrides the validated value with an unvalidated one, bypassing @Min(1) constraints?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `transactionTimeout` is annotated with @DurationUnit(ChronoUnit.SECONDS) meaning string values like `1` are interpreted as 1 second, but if the binding accepts a raw Duration string like `PT0.001S` (1ms), it could bypass the @DurationMin(seconds=1) constraint and set an effectively zero transaction timeout, causing all balance parse transactions to immediately time out?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.window` to the minimum allowed value of 100ms (satisfying @DurationMin(millis=100L)), causing the batch window check to trigger catch-up batching mode almost immediately, forcing the importer to always operate in high-throughput batch mode and consuming excessive database resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any constraint on the `enabled` field (no @NotNull, no validation) to set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.enabled=false` via environment variable, silently disabling all balance file parsing and causing the mirror node to serve permanently stale account balance data to all downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Component bean registration of BalanceParserProperties to trigger Spring Actuator endpoints (if exposed) that expose the full bean state including all configuration values, leaking the exact retry, timeout, and batch configuration to an attacker for targeted exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `batchSize * 5L` in the constructor is computed using Java int-to-long promotion, but if batchSize is set to a value > Integer.MAX_VALUE / 5 via a config source that accepts long values, the multiplication could produce a value exceeding Long.MAX_VALUE, wrapping to a negative number and setting batch.maxItems to a negative value that bypasses @Min(1) validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.maxBackoff` to the minimum allowed value of 500ms (satisfying @DurationMin(millis=500L)) and `retry.multiplier` to 1, causing the retry backoff to never increase beyond 500ms regardless of the number of failures, resulting in a tight retry loop that hammers a failing database and causes sustained denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.minBackoff` equal to `retry.maxBackoff` (both at their minimum values), causing the retry backoff to be a fixed constant with no jitter or growth, enabling a thundering-herd attack where multiple importer instances all retry simultaneously and overwhelm the database?
